content.full_text:
October 8, 1990

HAND DELIVERED

Mr. Jeevan Ahuja
Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Eighth Floor
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Ahuja:

This is to request that you confirm that the staff, at the October 2, 1990, Commission meeting, and without objection from any Commissioner, has concluded that the Order dated September 28, 1990 issued by Judge Lawrence K. Karlton, in Service Employee's International Union, et al. v. FPPC, et al. CIV-5-89-433 LKK EKG, permits (1) persons and committees which make contributions to legislative candidates, and (2) persons and committees which make independent expenditures in support of or opposition to legislative candidates, to be governed by the provisions of Proposition 73 governing contributions received and contributions made to candidates, or independent expenditures made by such persons and committees.

Specifically, the Commission has concluded that the effect of the extant orders in the above-referenced case do not result in the viability or enforceability of any of the Proposition 68 provisions relative to contributions received, contributions made or independent expenditures made by such persons or committees.

Please confirm that my understanding is correct.

This request is made in my capacity as Treasurer of committees which have previously, and will in the future prior to November 6, 1990, both receive contributions from contributors, make contributions to legislative candidates, and also receive contributions for the purpose of making independent expenditures in support of or opposition to legislative candidates. These material facts can be supplemented if necessary.

Please treat this as an informal interim advice request, and as a request for an opinion of the Commission pursuant to Government Code section 83114.

Mr. Jeevan Ahuja  
October 8, 1990  
Page 2

Thank you for your assistance.

Sincerely,

CHARLES H. BELL, JR.

CHB:tw:deh  
5363.19

cc: Scott Hallabrin,  
    Acting General Counsel  
    Commissioners
